fields.name: cost_time_impact
fields.label: Cost / Time Impact
options: Affects cost or schedule (route to change event)

GET /energy/benchmark-status.message: No benchmarking provider configured. EUI and monthly trends are computed locally from meter readings; set ENERGY_STAR_PROVIDER + ENERGY_STAR_API_KEY to sync a property with EPA Portfolio Manager for the official 1-100 score.

tools.name: project_snapshot
tools.description: Cross-module status snapshot for a project: RFI/submittal/CO/punch/safety tallies, schedule + budget KPIs and the risk headline.

tools.name: list_records
tools.description: List records of a module (e.g. rfi, submittal, information_container) for a project.

tools.name: cde_status
tools.description: ISO 19650 CDE container discipline for a project.

tools.name: bim_kpi_scorecard
tools.description: The 10-category BIM KPI scorecard for a project.

tools.name: openbim_quality
tools.description: openBIM model quality (LOIN, IDS compliance, export health, bSDD) — needs a loaded model.

tools.name: standards_check
tools.description: Run a standards-compliance check (iso19650 | cobie | ids | uniclass) against the project's data; returns findings with clause references and recommendations.

tools.name: create_rfi
tools.description: Create an RFI (Request for Information) on a project.

tools.name: list_recipes
tools.description: The authoring-coverage matrix: every edit recipe an agent can drive with run_recipe, grouped by category with the IFC class each emits. No project needed.

tools.name: run_recipe
tools.description: Drive a GUID-stable authoring recipe (e.g. add_wall, add_column, set_pset) against the project's IFC, saving a new audited version — the same server path the UI uses. Params are validated by the authoring guardrails; the edit is undoable. Reconvert/publish happens via the normal publish flow, not this tool. See list_recipes for names + params.

tools.name: schedule_risk
tools.description: Monte Carlo schedule forecast over the CPM network: P10/P50/P80/P90 completion, per-activity criticality index, and delay drivers; calibrated by the team's pull-plan PPC.

tools.name: carbon_report
tools.description: Embodied-carbon (A1–A3) inventory per element off the model index, plus Buy Clean limit checks and a LEED material inventory. Needs a loaded model.

tools.name: permit_readiness
tools.description: Submission-readiness report over the code engines + drawing register: egress, approvability, code analysis, and sheet-series coverage. Needs a loaded model.

tools.name: drawing_qa
tools.description: Drawing-set QA review: set integrity (duplicate/gap numbers, titleblock), issuance hygiene, and model cross-checks — every finding cited to its sheet. Runs without a model (register-only) and adds the cross-checks when one exists.

tools.name: model_quantities
tools.description: Measured quantity takeoff rolled up by discipline: reinforcement tonnage, MEP linear runs + fitting counts, structural volume, wall/slab areas. Needs a model.

tools.name: computed_schedules
tools.description: The door / window / room schedules computed straight from the model (marks, sizes, levels) — the same data the A-601 sheet prints. Needs a model.

tools.name: clash_results
tools.description: Geometric clash detection over the source model (element-pair intersections with volumes + GUIDs). Needs a model.

tools.name: code_violations
tools.description: Computed occupancy-load + egress-capacity findings (IBC 1004/1005) from the model's spaces and doors, citing sections — pre-check facts, not a certified review. Needs a model with IfcSpaces.

GET /mcp/tools.note: Run the stdio MCP server and point Claude Desktop / Cursor at it to drive the project by natural language. The MCP SDK (pip install 'mcp[cli]') is optional.

market_signal.headline: Two-speed market — data centres & advanced manufacturing hot; residential & commercial cold.

GET /market/snapshot.source: Seed defaults: Turner & Townsend Global Construction Market Intelligence 2026 (public headline figures) — editable; override with your own current rates.

GET /projects/76340c2f-38cf-4d12-85a7-87.note: Percentage-of-completion driven by cost-to-cost. Over-billing is a contract liability (billings in excess of costs & earnings); under-billing is a contract asset (costs & earnings in excess of billings) and a cash-flow drag. Retainage is tracked separately.

model.note: Physical progress (installed model elements ÷ total, by IFC GlobalId) vs cost-to-cost POC. A large gap flags over/under-billing risk or a cost / productivity variance — cost running ahead of physical progress is the classic front-loaded-billing signal.

GET /wip/portfolio.note: Under-billed jobs (contract assets) tie up cash; over-billed jobs (contract liabilities) have borrowed against future work. Sorted by under-billing.

GET /projects/76340c2f-38cf-4d12-85a7-87.note: Percentage-of-completion recognizes revenue as it's earned, not billed. The contract asset (under-billings) and liability (over-billings) reconcile earned to billed on the balance sheet; retainage receivable is carried separately from AR.

GET /contractor-statements/portfolio.note: Company-wide percentage-of-completion. Net contract working capital = (under-billings + retainage) − over-billings − AP; persistently negative signals a cash squeeze even when profitable.

accounts.name: Contract Asset — Costs in Excess of Billings
Contract Liability — Billings in Excess of Costs

entries.memo: POC revenue recognition — costs in excess of billings

lines.account: 1300 Contract Asset — Costs in Excess of Billings

GET /projects/76340c2f-38cf-4d12-85a7-87.note: Double-entry job cost + billing + WIP POC adjustment. Contract Revenue nets to earned (billed + under-billings − over-billings).

accounts.account: Contract Asset — Costs in Excess of Billings

GET /projects/76340c2f-38cf-4d12-85a7-87.note: Trial balance — total debits must equal total credits. Sourced from the double-entry journal (job cost + billing + WIP adjustment).
Cost traceable to IFC elements by GlobalId. Tag cost records with the model elements they pay for to raise coverage — then billing and cost are defensible against the model.

Weekly concurrent resource units per trade (histogram) + cumulative units and cost (S-curves). Over-allocation flags weeks above the availability cap. Add resource assignments (activity + cost code + units + rate) to cost-load the schedule.

suggestions.action: shift within 93-day float to smooth the peak
shift within 16-day float to smooth the peak
shift within 16-day float to smooth the peak
shift within 1-day float to smooth the peak

GET /projects/76340c2f-38cf-4d12-85a7-87.note: Smoothing candidates: over-allocated work with positive CPM float can move without delaying the finish. Assignments on the critical path (no float) can only be leveled by extending the schedule or raising the cap.

recommended.guidance: Mid (25–55%): Earned Schedule with SPI is most accurate; watch CPI trend.

totals.note: EV = schedule % × budget (control account = cost code). AC from posted direct costs. Forecasts are shown as a family; the best EAC is stage-dependent.

earned_schedule.note: Earned Schedule stays meaningful at completion (unlike dollar SPI, which → 1.0 whether or not the job finished on time). SPI(t) < 1 = behind; forecast_finish from IEAC(t).

GET /projects/76340c2f-38cf-4d12-85a7-87.note: PV is the full planned baseline; EV and AC run to the data date.
Earned Schedule stays meaningful at completion (unlike dollar SPI, which → 1.0 whether or not the job finished on time). SPI(t) < 1 = behind; forecast_finish from IEAC(t).

Model EV = installed % (field-verified elements) × BAC — the units-complete method from the model. If schedule EV exceeds model EV materially, reported progress is running ahead of physical installation (often a front-loaded SOV). No field verification recorded yet — model EV is not yet a check on reported progress.
CPI/SPI across captured snapshots. Capture one per reporting period to build the trend; a falling line means efficiency is deteriorating.

sources.label: Senior debt (65% LTC, bound by LTC)

risks.text: Forecast is over budget by $5,826,592 — review pending change orders and committed costs.
1 item(s) are overdue and need immediate attention.
1 change order(s) pending approval may impact cost/schedule.
2 open RFI(s) awaiting response could delay dependent work.
2 open safety item(s) — verify resolution before proceeding.
1 open punchlist item(s) remain for closeout.

constraints.basis: 16,000 SF footprint (lot coverage) x 20 floors (height 240ft / 12ft floors)

GET /projects/76340c2f-38cf-4d12-85a7-87.note: Site EUI (kBtu/sf/yr) annualized over the months with readings; standard conversion factors (kWh 3.412, therm 100). Water is tracked in gallons, not energy.

FCI = (deferred maintenance + capital renewal due) / current replacement value. Deferred = open deficiency costs; renewal = replacement value of elements past their useful life. Resolved elements leave the backlog. Lower FCI = healthier facility.

GET /fca/portfolio.note: Facility Condition Index per project, worst-first. The highest-FCI buildings carry the largest deferred backlog relative to their replacement value — fund those first.

GET /projects/76340c2f-38cf-4d12-85a7-87.note: Design Flood Elevation = Base Flood Elevation + freeboard (ASCE 24 minimum by Flood Design Class when not entered). Assets installed below the DFE are flagged to be elevated or flood-proofed. Enter installed elevations on Asset Register items.

Rational Method: Q = C·i·A (runoff coefficient × rainfall intensity in/hr × area in acres). Detention volume ≈ C × storm depth × area. Enter rainfall intensity/depth from the local IDF curve for the chosen return period.

Flag weather-sensitive activities so exposed work is sequenced out of the wet/freeze season; log site-weather hazards with controls; weather-delay days roll up from the daily reports' weather-impact field.

factors: Site in a FEMA Special Flood Hazard Area (1%-annual-chance floodplain).
1 asset(s) installed below the Design Flood Elevation.
GET /projects/76340c2f-38cf-4d12-85a7-87.note: Physical climate-risk rating rolls up flood-plain exposure, at-risk assets, open site-weather hazards and logged weather-delay days. Feeds the ESG summary.

Recurring replacements from the asset register (install + expected life) plus open capital-plan items; costs escalated at the given inflation rate. Suggested level contribution is the flat annual amount that keeps the reserve balance non-negative.

Gross-up applies to occupancy-variable recoverable expenses only, up to the stated occupancy; fixed expenses (taxes, insurance) pass through at actual. Estimated payments = lease recovery_psf x rentable_sf.

ghg.note: Scope 1 = on-site fuel (natural gas); Scope 2 = purchased electricity/steam/cooling at the configured grid factor. Set AEC_GRID_KGCO2E_PER_KWH to your eGRID subregion.

factors: Site in a FEMA Special Flood Hazard Area (1%-annual-chance floodplain).
1 asset(s) installed below the Design Flood Elevation.
physical_risk.note: Physical climate-risk rating rolls up flood-plain exposure, at-risk assets, open site-weather hazards and logged weather-delay days. Feeds the ESG summary.

GET /projects/76340c2f-38cf-4d12-85a7-87.note: CDE states per ISO 19650: WIP -> Shared -> Published -> Archived. Metadata completeness = containers with type, discipline, originator, suitability and revision all set.
A compliant appointment carries at least an EIR, a BEP, and (for the operational phase) an AIR. Missing core requirements are flagged.
Ten information-management KPI categories graded from CDE, model quality and the issue/asset/closeout records. Categories with no inputs show 'n/a' rather than a guess.
Data-drop acceptance mirrors an owner's handover checklist against the AIR: requirements, taggable asset data, as-builts, O&M and an accepted certificate.

dpp.note: Digital Product Passport is an emerging EU requirement (ESPR / revised CPR, phasing 2028-30): GS1 product ID + EPD/environmental + manufacturer traceability.

GET /projects/76340c2f-38cf-4d12-85a7-87.note: Twin readiness (ISO 23247): assets linked to a building system and mapped to a sensor/telemetry point, so live data can flow against a persistent asset identity.
Vendors needing a compliance nudge (expiring/expired/missing COI or unapproved prequal). Send before it blocks a bid invitation or pay application.

The program as a graph: nodes are spaces (area x quantity), edges are adjacency preferences. Net = leasable/occupiable use; the gross area and use mix feed the zoning->massing generator and the proforma.

sector.note: Slower momentum — softer investor confidence; more competitive bidding may be available.

GET /projects/76340c2f-38cf-4d12-85a7-87.source: Seed defaults: Turner & Townsend Global Construction Market Intelligence 2026 (public headline figures) — editable; override with your own current rates.

GET /projects/76340c2f-38cf-4d12-85a7-87.note: AI concept renders from a project's program + massing need an external image model. Enable AEC_RENDER_BRIDGE and connect a service that accepts a prompt and POSTs image references to /concept-render/ingest. The platform builds the prompt and stores results; it does not run or bundle a model, and fabricates nothing when off.
GET /projects/76340c2f-38cf-4d12-85a7-87.reference_adapter: docs/render-bridge.md — the HTTP contract + a runnable Python reference client you point at any image-generation service.

GET /projects/76340c2f-38cf-4d12-85a7-87.note: Last Planner phase board: work is pulled backward from the milestone, made ready by removing constraints, committed weekly, then scored by PPC. Every trade edits its own sticky notes; PPC = completed ÷ committed.

Last Planner reliability metrics: TMR = tasks made ready ÷ planned (are we clearing constraints ahead?); perfect-handoff = predecessor done and successor ready ÷ hand-offs; PPC trend = completion reliability week over week; variance Pareto = the top reasons work misses. Target PPC ≥ 80%.

loaded_model.note: STEP (IFC-SPF) schema read from the file header.

ifc5.note: IFC5 / IFCX / ifcJSON DATA is parsed into the model index (elements + properties) — analytics, audits and exports work on it now. Geometry RENDERING lands when web-ifc / Fragments ship IFC5 support upstream.

GET /projects/76340c2f-38cf-4d12-85a7-87.note: Streaming line-scan of the STEP text — header + entity-type histogram without a full ifcopenshell parse. For a quick model summary / pre-flight sizing.
Grouped + aggregated over the property index. Use saved views or pass group_by / agg=sum&quantity=<QtoName> / filters.
Achieved LOD inferred from LOIN facet completeness; capped at LOD 400 (LOD 500 is a verified as-built assertion, not a model read).
Target LOD by stage / discipline / element category. With no targets registered the RIBA/AIA stage defaults apply (SD LOD200 -> DD LOD300 -> CD LOD350 -> Construction LOD400 -> As-built LOD500).
Opaque assemblies vs IECC 2021 minimum R; fenestration vs maximum U, by climate zone. Representative prescriptive values — a first-pass screen, not a stamped energy model.
MEP elements counted off the model by IFC class (ducts, pipes, terminals, equipment, electrical). Pair with the equipment register for the engineered schedule.

container.note: e.g. DR_A_GroundFloorPlan_P01_2026-07-05 — revision-controlled; approved files are never overwritten.
e.g. DR_A_GroundFloorPlan_P01_2026-07-05 — revision-controlled; approved files are never overwritten.

GET /projects/76340c2f-38cf-4d12-85a7-87.note: Interning stores each distinct string once and references it by index — psets repeat the same keys/values across thousands of elements, so the columnar form is far smaller in RAM and streams to Parquet for DuckDB/pandas analytics.

GET /projects/76340c2f-38cf-4d12-85a7-87.disclaimer: Pre-check / design assist computed from the IFC — not a certified code review or a substitute for the authority having jurisdiction. Travel-distance and performance-based egress are out of scope.

GET /projects/76340c2f-38cf-4d12-85a7-87.note: Compare this signature across renders; a change means the 2D drawings are stale and should be regenerated (they render live from the model on request).
Health of the document set: names against the standard, coverage of required folders, revision control, and CDE-state spread. Superseded revisions are retained for audit.
Options compared on program + economics; best-in-class named per metric. Promote one to 'selected' to set the project's design direction (its drawing set becomes current).
Approved / preferred / prohibited assemblies, materials and products. The generator and authoring honor these; the model is audited against them.

ruleset.note: Approved / preferred / prohibited assemblies, materials and products. The generator and authoring honor these; the model is audited against them.

GET /projects/76340c2f-38cf-4d12-85a7-87.note: Elements flagged when their type/material matches a prohibited standard, or (when an approved set is declared) match nothing approved.
initial
Model integrity (complementary to LOIN/IDS data checks): duplicate GUIDs, orphaned elements, overlapping duplicates, unenclosed spaces, blank names, wrong-storey placement.

lenses.headline: 0.0% verified in place vs 2.2% claimed (trust gap 2.2)

GET /projects/76340c2f-38cf-4d12-85a7-87.note: A composite of the model-quality checks (hygiene, ISO 19650 KPIs, clash coordination, verified-as-built, and code/permit readiness). Each lens links to the tool that acts on it; lenses with no inputs show 'n/a' and are excluded from the score rather than guessed.
exact match — the export re-serializes the model with no change
Shared-coordinates basis for federation + BIM-to-field setout. LoGeoRef per BSI: 50 = projected CRS + map conversion; 40 = map conversion; 20 = site lat/long; 10 = elevation only; 0 = none.

GET /projects/76340c2f-38cf-4d12-85a7-87.disclaimer: PRELIMINARY lateral analysis for early coordination — ASCE 7 Equivalent Lateral Force (seismic) + simplified directional MWFRS (wind), distributed to story forces. NOT a full lateral design: no torsion / accidental eccentricity, no modal/response-spectrum analysis, with a preliminary §12.12 story-drift screen and a §12.3.2.1 torsional-irregularity flag (only when story stiffness / end displacements are supplied). No P-delta, no modal/response-spectrum analysis, no soil-structure interaction. All lateral system design must be performed and stamped by a licensed professional engineer.

drift.method: ASCE 7-22 §12.8.6 design story drift vs §12.12.1 allowable (Table 12.12-1)
drift.note: allowable Δa only — supply story_stiffness_kip_in or target_elastic_drift_ratio for a demand check

GET /projects/76340c2f-38cf-4d12-85a7-87.message: No analytical model found — run the derive_analytical recipe first.

GET /projects/76340c2f-38cf-4d12-85a7-87.prose: 20 elements (6 slab, 2 column, 2 covering, 2 footing, 1 beam, 1 railing) across 6 storey(s), 6 space(s). MEP: 3 system(s) — hvac (1), plumbing (1), electrical (1). 7 model-hygiene issue(s).

checks.note: no design flow on the run — size check skipped
no design flow on the run — size check skipped
provide a conductor fill ratio to check NEC 392.22 (≤ 50%)

GET /projects/76340c2f-38cf-4d12-85a7-87.disclaimer: PRELIMINARY MEP size check for early coordination — flow velocity from the authored nominal size + design flow against accepted velocity/fill limits (ASHRAE low-velocity air, erosion-limit water, NEC 392 tray fill). NOT a full hydraulic/thermal design (no pressure-loss balancing, no diversity, no acoustics). All final MEP sizing must be performed and stamped by a licensed professional engineer.

GET /projects/76340c2f-38cf-4d12-85a7-87.citation: NFPA 13 — maximum protection area per sprinkler (standard spray, unobstructed)
GET /projects/76340c2f-38cf-4d12-85a7-87.note: Coverage pre-check only (head count vs area ÷ max coverage) — not a hydraulic calculation, spacing check, or obstruction/beam-rule review.
GET /projects/76340c2f-38cf-4d12-85a7-87.verify: Confirm hazard classification, spacing and hydraulics with a fire-protection engineer.

GET /projects/76340c2f-38cf-4d12-85a7-87.note: Per-cost-code reconciliation: budget vs committed (subcontracts/POs) vs actual (direct costs) vs billed (sub invoices). Buyout margin = budget − committed (projected); variance = budget − actual (remaining). Over-committed / over-budget codes are flagged worst-first.

Allowance vs. actual across the selections log. Delta = actual − allowance: over is an add to the owner (a change-order candidate), under is a credit. Priced = selections with an actual cost entered; approved = owner-signed selections.

Maintainable assets derived from the IFC (serviceable equipment / terminals / controls / transport, subtype-resolved; ducts/pipes/fittings excluded). GUID-keyed — seed the asset_register module from this, then hang preventive maintenance (pm_schedule) off it.

Procurement equipment schedule derived from the IFC: procurable units grouped by class + type into RFQ line-items with a quantity + representative spec (from the model's Psets). Ducts/pipes/fittings and controls are excluded. Feed this into a buyout package / RFQ.
Occupancy capacity per space at the given area-per-person standard, rolled up by type. Pure arithmetic over the modelled net floor areas — no sensors.

hazards_to_verify: Seismic design parameters (Ss, S1) from the site's seismic hazard map
Basic wind speed for the risk category from the wind hazard map
Ground snow load from the snow-load map
Flood design elevation from the local flood map (FIRM or equivalent)
Energy-code climate zone from the energy code's climate map

GET /projects/76340c2f-38cf-4d12-85a7-87.reframe_prompt: Name what this asset actually is, stripped of marketing — the real use drives the comps, the tenants, and the buyer.

steps.title: Place & context
steps.why: Location decides which code governs, who approves, and what the loads are.
gaps: Jurisdiction (AHJ / state) so code editions + loads resolve
Georeference the model (site lat/long) to derive climate + hazards

steps.title: Program & highest-and-best-use
steps.why: Name what the asset actually is, and confirm it's the right use here — legally, physically, financially.

steps.title: Feasibility & the money
steps.why: Does it pencil? Sources & uses, development budget, and returns are the spine every decision hangs from.

steps.title: Regulatory path
steps.why: Zoning → building code, fire, energy, accessibility, structural loads, MEP — sequenced with a timeline.

steps.title: Design integration
steps.why: Architecture, structure, envelope, MEP resolved as one system — clashes are cheapest to fix in the model.

steps.title: Delivery strategy
steps.why: How to buy, build, sequence, and control it: delivery method, estimate, schedule, procurement, long-leads.

steps.title: Risk
steps.why: Name the risks, then allocate each to whoever can best carry it, and mitigate what remains.

findings.label: Risks identified on the risk board

steps.title: Handover & life
steps.why: A building is a 50-year cash-flow and carbon liability — commissioning, close-out, operations, disposition.

data.deliverables:
Business case
Site + zoning feasibility
Test fit
Order-of-magnitude proforma

data.subject: 1 Preparation & Briefing — Pre-Design / Programming
data.deliverables:
Project brief / program
EIR (Exchange Information Requirements)
BEP
Feasibility studies

data.subject: 2 Concept Design — Schematic Design (SD)
data.deliverables:
Massing + concept model
Design narrative
Outline specs
SD estimate (Class 4)

data.subject: 3 Spatial Coordination — Design Development (DD)
data.deliverables:
Coordinated architecture + structure + MEP
Developed specs
DD estimate (Class 3)
Clash-free model

data.subject: 4 Technical Design — Construction Documents (CD)
data.deliverables:
Construction drawings
Project manual / specifications
IDS-validated model
CD estimate (Class 2)

data.subject: 5 Manufacturing & Construction — Bidding + Construction Administration (CA)
data.deliverables:
Bid / award
RFIs + submittals
ASIs / bulletins / change orders
Site observation reports

data.subject: 6 Handover — Substantial Completion / Closeout
data.deliverables:
Punch list + architect sign-off
G704 substantial completion
As-built / record model
COBie + O&M turnover

data.subject: 7 Use — Post-Occupancy / Operations
data.deliverables:
Warranty tracking
Commissioning close-out
Post-occupancy evaluation
Operations & maintenance
